message.content:
Certainly! Below is the JSON with the "code" key added to each relevant entry where lines of code from the final solution are connected to the text of that entry. Unnecessary entries without corresponding code have been excluded:

```json
[
    {
        "text": "with my helper function which i haven't",
        "code": [
            "class Solution:",
            "def reverseKGroup(self, head: Optional[ListNode], k: int) -> Optional[ListNode]:",
            "dummy = ListNode(0, head)",
            "groupPrev = dummy"
        ]
    },
    {
        "text": "i want to take this and append it to",
        "code": [
            "while True:",
            "kth = self.getKth(groupPrev, k)",
            "if not kth:",
            "break"
        ]
    },
    {
        "text": "our merged lists",
        "code": [
            "groupNext = kth.next",
            "prev, curr = kth.next, groupPrev.next",
            "while curr != groupNext:",
            "tmp = curr.next",
            "curr.next = prev",
            "prev = curr",
            "curr = tmp"
        ]
    },
    {
        "text": "variable over here where we are storing",
        "code": [
            "tmp = groupPrev.next",
            "groupPrev.next = kth",
            "groupPrev = tmp",
            "return dummy.next"
        ]
    },
    {
        "text": "i've seen people do it a different way",
        "code": [
            "def getKth(self, curr, k):",
            "while curr and k > 0:",
            "curr = curr.next",
            "k -= 1",
            "return curr"
        ]
    }
]
```

In this alignment, several segments of the transcript related to planning and logic have been matched with the appropriate blocks of code. Note how each segment matches logically to sections of the solution.
